data.title: Still Feeling Bearish? Best Defensive Stocks To Buy Now
data.description:
Investors Shift from ‘Raging Bulls’ to Bears

Warren Buffett, The Oracle of Omaha, has long preached that gains and losses do not exist in equilibrium. For example, if a holding loses 50% of its value, a 100% gain is necessary to break even. While chasing investment fads can often lead to devastating losses, protecting your current investment portfolio is the key to building long-term wealth. In tumultuous times like these, I often return to Warren Buffett’s golden rule: "Never lose money" and "Never forget rule number one." While it may sound overly simplistic, it perfectly captures his value-driven, risk-averse philosophy.

Buffett's capital preservation strategy continues to resonate as the S&P 500 and Nasdaq rebounded Wednesday after retreating fourconsecutive weeks. A recent survey by Bank of America shows that investors have made their “biggest ever” retreat from U.S. equities in March, with allocations plummeting 40% before month-end. The mass exodus comes as President Donald Trump’s trade war, increasing geopolitical tensions, and slowing economic growth have pushed traders’ resilience to the limit. Nearly 70% of respondents said that the idea of “US exceptionalism,” which initially boosted the S&P 500 and Nasdaq to record highs following Trump’s election victory, has peaked. “At the beginning of the year investors were all raging bulls,” said Elyas Galou, a senior investment strategist at BofA, “but they are ending the winter as bears for sure.”

A closer look at the CBOE Volatility Index (VIX), Wall Street’s fear gauge, shows that its 20-day moving average is at its highest since April 2023, surpassing levels seen during the brief but extreme market volatility of early August 2024. While the VIX’s highest close during that period was 38.56 (and a high of 65.73), recent volatility has been gradual with a more extended build-up period.

Wall Street’s Fear Gauge Nears Peak Levels

Further signs of rising financial stress are evident in corporate debt. Since January 2025, the spread between junk and investment-grade yields has widened by over 0.6 percentage points, recently reaching its highest level since September 2024. Analysts monitor this spread as a key gauge of perceived credit risk. During periods of economic uncertainty, investors typically demand a higher risk premium, causing the spread to widen.

Bond Yield Spreads Widen, Revealing Higher Credit Risk

Feeling bearish? As BofA’s Galou observed, the transition from bull to bear is only natural as markets continue to roil. Remaining diligent in your investment strategy and resisting panic selling are key. Instead, prioritize downside protection by focusing on defensive stocks with strong fundamentals.

Investing in Non-Cyclical Stocks

In periods of uncertainty, it may be a good time to pull back on more speculative positions in lieu of non-cyclical defensive stocks, which tend to outperform the market during an economic slowdown. Defensive companies typically hail from sectors that generate constant demand regardless of the economic climate—necessities such as food, consumer staples, healthcare services, and utilities. Real estate investment trusts ((REITs)) also tend to perform well in a downmarket due to their stable dividend income, tangible asset backing, and inflation-fighting properties. Their required high dividend payouts provide consistent cash flow, while real estate holdings help preserve value. They also tend to be less correlated to equities, adding portfolio diversification. For more on REITs and their volatility-hedging powers, read about my top five REITs here.

Identifying strong defensive stocks during a downturn begins with selecting stocks with solid underlying financials that have continued to perform as more growth-oriented stocks have fallen in value. Defensive companies typically possess these key features:

  • They are well-established, with solid business models and a proven history of strong earnings growth.

  • They hail from non-cyclical sectors with perennial demand that often doesn’t waver in recessionary periods.

  • They possess strong balance sheets with minimal debt.

  • Many offer stable dividend payments.

The SA Quant algorithm separates weak companies from strong ones based on their historical financials, analysts' consensus growth estimates, and hundreds of financial metrics. Using these insights, I have identified my top stocks across four non-cyclical sectors: healthcare, utilities, consumer staples, and REITs.

All four recommendations possess Strong Buy ratings, powerful momentum, and attractive valuations, among other positive Quant Factor Grades. When it comes to volatile markets, I also like to reference our Quant dividend grades, which rank stocks by their dividend safety, growth, and consistency to find companies that can provide extra stability.

Let’s dive into the top defensive stock from each category.

1. Healthcare Pick: Exelixis (EXEL)

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/20/2025): 22 out of 1013

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/20/2025): 10 out of 498

  • Market Capitalization: $10.4B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Forward Dividend Yield: N/A

Exelixis (EXEL) is an Alameda, California-based biotech company focused on developing new medicines for difficult-to-treat cancers. Included within SA’s list of Top Defensive Stocks earlier this week, shares in the company have rallied more than 57% over the past 12 months and has remained in the green over the past month amid volatile markets. That momentum and its strong financial performance have helped land it as my top healthcare pick.

The company delivered strong growth in Q4, which included an adjusted EPS of $0.55 compared to analysts’ estimate of $0.49. Delivering shareholder value, EXEL announced an additional $500M stock repurchase program before December 31, 2025. Its main product is Cabometyx, a drug for renal cell carcinoma and other cancers, which generated $515M during the fourth quarter of 2024, a 20% increase from the previous quarter. EXEL’s cutting-edge cancer drug Zanzalintinib has also emerged as a powerful growth engine, targeting $5B in sales by 2033.

EXEL Q4 2024 Presentation

While the biopharma company’s long-term success hinges heavily on the success of Zanzalintinib, its short-term prospects are bright.As SA analyst Biotech Beast writes:

In the short term, I can certainly rate EXEL a buy given the favorable valuation on P/E metrics, continued growth of the existing cabozantinib franchise and prospects for approval of cabozantinib in a new indication in April.

In addition to market-crushing performance, EXEL’s Quant Factor Grades are equally impressive given the current market sell-off.

EXEL Stock Momentum & Valuation

EXEL’s ‘A+’ Momentum Grade truly excels compared to its peers, with three-month price performance of 8% compared to the -8% sector median.

The company is trading at an attractive valuation compared to the broader market, with a price-to-earnings-growth (PEG) ratio ((TTM) of 0.1, a near 87% discount from the sector, and a price-to-earnings (P/E) ratio ((TTM) of 23.55 vs. the sector’s 32.09.

Impressive year-over-year return on equity growth of 430.82%, a significant difference from the sector’s -2.34%, indicates a healthy return on equity thus far. Meanwhile, an ‘A+’-rated EPS long-term growth rate of 32.96% over the next three to five years suggests that analysts agree that EXEL possesses a strong growth pipeline.

EXEL is also highly profitable, with an ‘A+’ grade in the category. 13 analysts also appear confident, offering positive EPS revisions over the last 90 days compared to 3 negative revisions.

Like EXEL, my next selection has the perfect blend of powerful momentum, a robust balance sheet, and a strong growth outlook to help it navigate the current bear market.

2. Consumer Staples Pick: The Clorox Company (CLX)

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/19/2025): 1 out of 184

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 1/19/2025): 1 out of 13

  • Market Capitalization: $17.9B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Forward Dividend Yield: 3.08%

#1 Quant-ranked stock in the Consumer Staples Sector and Household Products Industry, Clorox Company (CLX)’s consistent dividend yield and improving financials could be just the thing to polish your portfolio in time for spring cleaning.

As my top consumer staples pick for market volatility, its eponymous bleach-based cleaning products, and popular household brands,including Pine-Sol, Brita, and Glad have captured market share. CLX’s dominance over its sector helped it become a top performer during the COVID-19 pandemic and continues to help it navigate the current market environment.

Despite solid FYQ2 financial results overall, reporting in-line adjusted EPS for Q2 of $1.40, a 15% fall in net sales dampened Wall Street’s outlook as CLX’s Quant Rating dropped from a Strong Buy to a Hold in mid-February. CLX’s shares are down around 9.5% since it reported earnings on February 3, but valuation, growth, and momentum improvements have fueled a return to Strong Buy status in the following weeks.

CLX also increased gFY25 guidance to $6.95- $7.35, while diluted EPS is expected to fall between $5.52 and $5.92. CEO Linda Rendle noted on its latest earnings call that it has also vowed to improve its performance in terms of falling sales by spending heavily on advertising in the second half of 2025 amid new product launches.

In the meantime, CLX’s modest but reliable 3.4% forward dividend yield makes it a great option for value-oriented income investors–the company recently declared a $1.22 dividend in line with past payments.

CLX Stock Quant Breakdown

Despite its overall ‘C-’ Valuation Grade, CLX’s forward PEG of 2 still comes at a 9% discount to the sector, underscoring a fair valuation amid inflated consumer staples shares across the sector. Its trailing PEG of 0.08, a 90% discount to CLX’s peers, further suggests that the company has been priced competitively.

In addition to ‘A+’ Profitability, CLX demonstrates impressive forward-looking growth, with forward EPS GAAP growth of around 80%—an 876% difference to the sector median—reflecting its improving performance outlook. That optimism is shared by 19 analysts who have issued positive EPS revisions over the past three months, compared to 0 negative revisions.

Aside from solid Quant Factor Grades, CLX truly shines in terms of dividend growth and consistency, growing its dividend for an astounding 47 consecutive years.

Clorox is a mainstay consumer staple investment that provides investors with an attractive, stable dividend. My next selection offers similarly reliable shareholder payouts.

3. Utilities Pick: Exelon Corporation (NYSE: EXC)

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/20/25): 2 out of 105

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/20/25): 1 out of 40

  • Market Capitalization: $44.7B

  • Quant Rating: Buy

  • Forward Dividend Yield: 3.6%

Utilities tend to outperform other sectors during economic slowdowns. The top-ranked Electric Utilities Stock, Exelon Corporation (EXC), has proven its ability to withstand volatility. The Chicago-based electric company has benefited from a surge in data center demand by artificial intelligence technology companies, and has also strategically aligned itself to profit from the ongoing energy transition. Its 2024 investment plan targets 34B through 2027 for grid improvement, renewable energy, and infrastructure. EXC’s strong Q4’24 earnings results beat expectations with adjusted operating earnings of $0.64 per share, underlining its long-term earnings per share growth target of 5-7% through 2027.

Shares in the company have risen around 18% YTD, crushing the S&P’s -4.5% return during that period.

At the same time, projected EPS GAAP growth of 6.2% offers a decent amount of safety, while its 5-7% adjusted operating earnings target supports stable dividend growth. “For 2025, we anticipate paying out a dividend of $1.60 per share, representing 5.2% growth over last year,” Chief Financial Officer Jeanne Jones noted on its latest earnings call.

That sustainable dividend growth and the positive Quant metrics discussed below make EXC a solid defensive pick for income-driven investors.

CLX Stock Valuation & Momentum

EXC exhibits a sturdy valuation: its forward P/E of 16.5 shows that it is currently undervalued compared to its historical performance, while its forward PEG of 2.8, a 4% difference from the sector median, shows that it currently goes for a fair price tag. Solid growth characteristics and forward dividend per share growth of 5.6%, reflect its steady upward trajectory. 11 positive EPS revisions from analysts over the past 90 days, strong profitability, and powerful momentum further reinforce the utility’s Strong Buy rating.

EXC has consecutively raised its dividend over the three years due to AI-fuelled demand and paid shareholders a dividend 23 years in a row with a five-year growth rate of over 8%, resulting in solid Quant Dividend Scores across the board.

My next selection offers the most attractive dividend yield of them all.

4. REIT Pick: Broadstone Net Lease, Inc. (NYSE: BNL)

  • Quant Sector Ranking (as of 3/20/25): 2 out of 173

  • Quant Industry Ranking (as of 3/20/25): 1 out of 14

  • Market Capitalization: $3.35B

  • Quant Rating: Strong Buy

  • Forward Dividend Yield: 6.7%

Up around 14% over the past year and 10% over the past month, Broadstone Net Lease, Inc. (BNL) continues to climb even as the broader stock market is getting crushed, showcasing why REITs make for great defensive investments during a market downturn. With an incredible 6.7% forward dividend yield and forward price-to-FFO ratio of 10.55x, among other income-generating qualities, BNL defends its place as the top Quant-rated Diversified REIT Stock.

The real estate group posted average Q4 2024 results that left investors wanting more. However, it achieved $1.43 AFFO per share for 2024 and secured $400M in total investments last year. The company anticipates reporting AFFO of between $1.45 and $1.49 per diluted share in 2025, indicating stronger growth for its portfolio of office buildings, healthcare centers, and more.

BNL Stock Quant Breakdown

With one and three-year yield-on-cost ratios of 7.8% and 5.4%, respectively, BNL boasts a fantastic dividend metrics with an overall ‘A-’ in the category. Analysts predict it will continue to grow in the next few years, with a 7.33% consensus ratio for December 2026, making it a compelling choice for value-inclined investors—particularly given the current downturn.

BNL Dividend Scorecard (as of 3/20/25)

BNL also touts great dividend consistency, with 4 years of consistent dividend growth—a 950% difference to the sector—and a three-year CAGR dividend growth rate of 4%.

When it comes to valuation, BNL is ahead of the curve. Its forward price-to-funds from operations (P/FFO) ratio of 11 represents an 18.4% discount to the sector median, while its forward price-to-adjusted funds from operations (P/AFFO) ratio of 11.5 comes at a 23.6% discount. SA analyst Pearl Gray Equity and Research notes:

Despite a decline in FFO per share, largely due to asset sales, Broadstone's forward price-to-FFO ratio of 10.55x and 7.46% dividend yield remain attractive.

The REIT has also demonstrated solid profitability with an AFFO/Total Revenue (TTM) ratio of nearly 70%, a 73% difference from the sector, and a gross profit margin (TTM) of 94%.

Conclusion: Defensive Stocks and Dividends for The Win

Tariffs, trade wars, and slowing economic growth can be enough to turn even the most bullish of investors into bears. But instead of panicking, be like Buffett and focus on capital preservation by considering defensive stocks with great fundamentals.

Non-cyclical stocks from the four sectors discussed in this piece–healthcare, utilities, consumer staples, and REITs–can be a great addition to a well-rounded, diverse portfolio. They provide peace of mind and most offer consistent dividends,even in a market sell-off.

For more stock recommendations like these, consider using Seeking Alpha's ETF Ratings Screener tool to help find stocks that achieve diversification into the desired sectors you like. Alternatively, if you're looking for a select number of Quant Strong Buy recommendations on a monthly basis, you might want to explore Alpha Picks.
